description: Self-reported race, OMB categories. Multi-select permitted; separator '|'.
Self-reported ancestry at finer granularity than OMB race (country of origin, ethnic group, etc.).
Computational genetic ancestry estimate at the 1000 Genomes superpopulation level.
Computational genetic ancestry estimate at finer-grained subpopulation level. Default vocabulary: GP2 11-class taxonomy.

metadata_.expansion_notes: AAC=African Admixed/Caribbean; AJ=Ashkenazi Jewish; CAH=Complex Admixed Hispanic; CAS=Central Asian; FIN=Finnish; MDE=Middle Eastern

description: Method used to estimate genetic ancestry. Required when any AncestryGenetic* field is populated.
Reference panel used as the basis for genetic ancestry estimation. Required when any AncestryGenetic* field is populated.

Globally unique identifier for the family/pedigree this individual belongs to. All individuals sharing a FamilyID are members of the same pedigree; individuals with no family enrollment have FamilyID equal to IndividualID (singleton family). Stable across the lifetime of the cohort. PLINK FAM file Column 1 maps directly to this field.

metadata_.singleton_convention: set FamilyID = IndividualID for individuals not enrolled with relatives
metadata_.rationale: Family-based association tests, linkage analyses, transmission disequilibrium tests, and variance-component models all require explicit family grouping. Without a stable FamilyID, every analysis tool reinvents the family-detection wheel from related-pair lists.

description: IndividualID of this individual's biological father, when known and present in the cohort. Null/missing when father is unknown or not enrolled. PLINK convention uses '0' for unknown; RoP uses null with a populated PaternalKnown flag instead, distinguishing 'father unknown' from 'father known but not in cohort'.

metadata_.null_meaning: father not enrolled in cohort or unknown; see PaternalKnown for disambiguation

description: IndividualID of this individual's biological mother, when known and present in the cohort. Same null/known semantics as PaternalIndividualID.

Whether biological paternity information is known. True when paternity is established; false when paternity is unknown; 'in-cohort' when known and the father is also enrolled (PaternalIndividualID populated); 'out-of-cohort' when known but father is not enrolled. Disambiguates the common cause of null PaternalIndividualID values.
Whether biological maternity information is known. Same semantics as PaternalKnown.

Whether this individual is a founder of the pedigree (no parents in the dataset). Founders contribute genetic material that is not derived from other dataset members. Required for variance-component models, IBD inference, and many family-based association methods. Computed automatically when PaternalIndividualID and MaternalIndividualID are known but recorded explicitly to avoid recomputation by every downstream tool.

metadata_.computation_rule: founder if both PaternalIndividualID and MaternalIndividualID are null or out-of-cohort; non-founder if at least one parent is in-cohort

description: Identifier for monozygotic (identical) twin or higher-order MZ multiple grouping. All MZ multiples sharing this identifier are genetically identical and should be treated as a single genetic unit for IBD-based and variance-component analyses while remaining distinct for environmental and longitudinal analyses. Null for non-twins and dizygotic twins.

metadata_.rationale: MZ twins share 100% of genome IBD; treating them as independent observations inflates effective sample size and biases standard error estimates.

description: Identifier for dizygotic (fraternal) twin or higher-order DZ multiple grouping. DZ multiples share an average of 50% of genome IBD (like full siblings) but were gestated together and share environmental exposures distinguishing them from non-twin siblings.

Relationship of this individual to the family proband (the individual who brought the family to clinical or research attention). Free-text encoding of standard kinship terms with structured form. The proband themselves carries the value 'proband'. Particularly important for ascertainment-aware analyses.

Reference to the canonical pedigree file representation when one exists. Format: PLINK FAM (.fam), GEDCOM (.ged), CSV with documented columns, or PED format. Allows downstream tools that prefer file-based pedigree input to retrieve the authoritative version rather than reconstructing from per-row anchors. Same URI scheme rules as Tier 8 DataAssetURI (DRS preferred).

Whether the individual is the product of a consanguineous union, when known. Affects expected runs-of-homozygosity and inflates the chance of recessive disease. Particularly relevant for population-specific cohorts (e.g., GP2 South Asian, MENA studies) where consanguinity rates are non-negligible.
